title: 怪屋
summary:
Brenda tells the narrator about her late husband, Aristide, whom she describes as a clever man who provided her with a home and happiness. She recounts their meeting at the Three-Leaf restaurant, where Aristide comforted her while she worked as a servant. She explains that she eventually discovered he was the wealthy "Leonidis Mr.," owning numerous businesses, and they married in a small London church. Brenda expresses her sadness over his death and denies killing him, claiming that others, including Roger and Philip, have treated her poorly. She notes that the family and Miss de Harvillan remained in Aristide's house despite their marriage.

Brenda also discusses Lawrence Brown, describing him as a sensitive and fragile man whom she tries to encourage. She expresses fear regarding Josephine, whom she finds odd and sneaky. When the narrator suggests that people might suspect Brenda and Lawrence of conspiring to poison Aristide due to a potential romantic motive, Brenda vehemently denies any such relationship.

Later, the narrator speaks with Sophia, expressing pity for Brenda. Sophia remains unsympathetic, arguing that Brenda is manipulative and that the family has no obligation to be kind to her. Sophia asserts that "Grandfather" was not deceived by Brenda but was instead orchestrating the situation. According to Sophia, Grandfather's plan involved the marriage and the hiring of Lawrence Brown as a tutor to provide Brenda with a controlled, "melancholic" romance to prevent her from engaging in outside scandals.
